title: Lady Gaga Wins Her First Grammy of the Night, As 'Abracadabra' Takes Best Dance Pop Recording
text: Lady Gaga's electro banger "Abracadabra" won the best dance pop recording Grammy on Sunday (Feb. 1) in Los Angeles. "Abracadabra" was also nominated for record of the year and song of the year, with those awards to be presented during the televised ceremony later that evening. Regarding the Record of the Year award specifically, Kendrick Lamar won Record of the Year. So while "Abracadabra" was nominated in the Record of the Year category, it did not win that particular award. Lady Gaga was up for seven Grammy Awards in total at the 2026 Grammys: Record Of The Year ("Abracadabra"), Album Of The Year (MAYHEM), Song Of The Year ("Abracadabra"), Best Pop Solo Performance ("Disease"), Best Pop Vocal Album (MAYHEM), Best Dance Pop Recording ("Abracadabra"), and Best Traditional Pop Vocal Album (Harlequin). She won Best Pop Vocal Album for MAYHEM, Best Dance Pop Recording for "Abracadabra" and Best Remixed Recording for Gesaffelstein's remix of "Abracadabra."
